issues.title: Create comprehensive seed data SQL for all core tables
issues.description: The seed directory is empty per PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P3. No seed data exists for any table. Without seed data, every page shows empty states and the app cannot be demonstrated to GCs. Need 2 orgs, 3 projects, 10 users, 25 RFIs, 15 submittals, 50 punch items, 30 daily logs, budget line items by CSI division, 5 change orders, and notifications.
issues.prompt: Create the file supabase/seed.sql with comprehensive construction seed data. Include: 2 organizations (Turner Construction, Hensel Phelps), 3 projects (Riverside Commercial Tower $42M, Metro Hospital Wing $28M, University Science Center $15M), 10 users with profiles across roles (owner, admin, project_manager, superintendent, subcontractor, field_engineer, safety_manager, viewer — use emails like mike.chen@turnergc.com with deterministic UUIDs 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 through 88888888-8888-8888-8888-888888888888), project_members linking users to projects with roles, 25 RFIs across statuses (open, under_review, answered, closed) with realistic construction titles like 'Structural beam depth at grid line F7' and priorities, 15 submittals with spec sections (03 30 00, 05 12 00, 09 29 00 etc), 50 punch_list_items across trades (Electrical, Drywall, Paint, HVAC, Plumbing) and locations (Floor 1 Room 101, Floor 3 Corridor B), 30 daily_logs with weather data and crew entries, budget_line_items for all 3 projects by CSI division (01 through 33) with realistic dollar amounts summing to contract values, 5 change_orders in different statuses with cost impacts, and notification records. Use INSERT statements with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for idempotency. All monetary values in integer cents. All dates in 2025-2026 range. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Budget endpoint missing — no getCostData or budget list API
issues.description: The projects.ts file imports getCostData from './budget' but this file likely has incomplete coverage. The Budget page acceptance criteria require: original budget by CSI division, approved changes, revised budget, committed cost, invoiced to date, cost to complete, projected final cost, and variance. The financial engine computeProjectFinancials is called but the actual budget CRUD endpoints (list budget items, create, update, get summary) need to be complete and follow the buildPaginatedQuery pattern.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/budget.ts, ensure the following functions exist and are fully implemented following the existing patterns from field.ts and people.ts: (1) getCostData(projectId) — fetches all budget_line_items for a project, returns array with csi_code, description, original_amount, approved_changes, revised_budget, committed_cost, actual_cost, projected_final, variance fields. BEFORE: function may be missing or incomplete. AFTER: Full implementation using supabase.from('budget_line_items').select('*').eq('project_id', projectId).order('csi_code') with assertProjectAccess guard and transformSupabaseError. (2) getBudgetSummary(projectId) — aggregates all line items into totals: total_original_budget, total_approved_changes, total_revised_budget, total_committed, total_actual, total_projected, total_variance, contingency_original, contingency_used, contingency_remaining. Compute these by summing getCostData results. (3) updateBudgetLineItem(projectId, itemId, updates) — updates a single budget line item with optimistic locking (check updated_at). (4) createBudgetLineItem(projectId, input) — inserts new line item. All functions must use assertProjectAccess, transformSupabaseError, and return properly typed results. Export all functions. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Notification queue table and dispatch endpoint missing for email notifications
issues.description: PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-5 requires email notifications. The notification_queue table does not exist in any migration. Without it, there is no way to queue, track, or retry email sends. This blocks the entire email notification feature which is a GC dealbreaker — RFIs sit unanswered for weeks without notifications.
issues.prompt: Create the file supabase/migrations/20250402000001_notification_queue.sql with the following schema: CREATE TABLE notification_queue (id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), project_id uuid not null references projects(id) on delete cascade, recipient_user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id), recipient_email text not null, template_name text not null, template_data jsonb not null default '{}', entity_type text, entity_id uuid, status text not null default 'pending' check (status in ('pending', 'processing', 'sent', 'failed', 'skipped')), attempts integer not null default 0, max_attempts integer not null default 3, sent_at timestamptz, error text, created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()); CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_status ON notification_queue(status, created_at) WHERE status = 'pending'; CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_recipient ON notification_queue(recipient_user_id, created_at DESC); CREATE INDEX idx_notification_queue_project ON notification_queue(project_id); ALTER TABLE notification_queue ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY 'Project members can view own notifications' ON notification_queue FOR SELECT USING (recipient_user_id = auth.uid()); CREATE POLICY 'Authenticated users can insert' ON notification_queue FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (project_id IN (SELECT pm.project_id FROM project_members pm WHERE pm.user_id = auth.uid())); Also create a notification_preferences table: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_preferences (id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid not null references auth.users(id) on delete cascade unique, rfi_assigned boolean default true, rfi_overdue boolean default true, submittal_status boolean default true, change_order_pending boolean default true, daily_log_reminder boolean default true, punch_item_assigned boolean default true, meeting_scheduled boolean default true, digest_enabled boolean default true, digest_time time default '19:00:00', timezone text default 'America/Denver', created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()); ALTER TABLE notification_preferences ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY 'Users manage own preferences' ON notification_preferences FOR ALL USING (user_id = auth.uid()); Add trigger for updated_at on both tables using the existing update_updated_at() function. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Activity feed batchFetchEntityLabels has unbounded parallel queries without concurrency limit
issues.description: batchFetchEntityLabels fires parallel supabase queries for each entity type (rfi, submittal, etc.) with no concurrency limit. With many entity types, this creates a thundering herd of requests. Also, the function only handles 'rfi' and 'submittal' types — missing change_orders, daily_logs, punch_items, meetings which are all common activity feed entities. This means most activity items show raw IDs instead of readable labels.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/activity.ts, in the batchFetchEntityLabels function: BEFORE: Only handles 'rfi' and 'submittal' entity types. Other entity types (change_order, daily_log, punch_item, meeting, drawing) show no label. AFTER: Add handlers for these additional entity types following the exact same pattern as the existing rfi/submittal blocks: (1) 'change_order' — query change_orders table, select 'id, change_order_number, title', label format 'CO-{number} {title}'. (2) 'daily_log' — query daily_logs table, select 'id, log_date', label format 'Daily Log {log_date}'. (3) 'punch_item' — query punch_list_items (or punch_items depending on actual table name in database.ts), select 'id, title, location', label format '{title} at {location}'. (4) 'meeting' — query meetings table, select 'id, title, date', label format '{title} ({date})'. (5) 'drawing' — query drawings table, select 'id, sheet_number, title', label format '{sheet_number} {title}'. Each block should push a promise to the fetches array, iterate results, set labels in labelMap, and cache with setCachedEntityLabel. Also wrap all Promise.all(fetches) to limit concurrency — use Promise.allSettled(fetches) instead of Promise.all to prevent one failure from blocking all labels. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Edit lock has no automatic cleanup for orphaned locks from crashed sessions
issues.description: The edit lock system relies on a 2-minute expiry with frontend renewal. However, if a user's browser crashes or loses network, the lock persists for 2 full minutes blocking other users. There is no server-side cleanup cron, no heartbeat verification, and no way for a project_manager to force-release a lock. Also, acquireEditLock does not record project_id, making it impossible to scope lock cleanup to a project.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/editLocks.ts, make these specific changes: (1) Add a forceReleaseLock function: export async function forceReleaseLock(entityType: string, entityId: string, requestingUserId: string): Promise<boolean>. This function should first verify the requesting user has project_manager or higher role (query project_members for the entity's project), then delete the lock row. Return true on success. This allows PMs to unblock stuck locks. (2) Add a getActiveLocks function: export async function getActiveLocks(projectId: string): Promise<Array<{entityType: string, entityId: string, lockedBy: string, lockedByName: string | null, expiresAt: string}>>. Query edit_locks joined with profiles where expires_at > now(). This powers a 'Current Locks' admin panel. (3) Add a cleanupExpiredLocks function: export async function cleanupExpiredLocks(): Promise<number>. Delete all rows where expires_at < now(). Return count of deleted rows. This should be called from an edge function on a 5-minute cron. (4) In acquireEditLock, before checking for existing locks, call cleanupExpiredLocks() to opportunistically clean stale locks. BEFORE: No force release, no listing, no cleanup. AFTER: Three new exported functions plus automatic cleanup on acquire. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Notification Dispatch Edge Function
invented_features.description: Create supabase/functions/notification-dispatch/index.ts that processes the notification_queue table every 30 seconds. For each pending notification, render the email template (RFI assigned, submittal approved, overdue alert etc.), send via Resend API, update status to sent/failed with retry logic. Include rate limiting (10 emails/second), dead letter queue for permanently failed sends, and daily digest aggregation. This is the P0-5 feature from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.

invented_features.title: Budget Variance Alert Triggers
invented_features.description: Create database triggers on budget_line_items that automatically queue notifications when a line item's projected_final exceeds revised_budget by more than 5%. Insert into notification_queue with template_name 'budget_variance_alert' and template_data containing CSI code, description, variance amount, and percentage. This gives CFOs real-time budget protection without manual monitoring.

competitive_intel: Procore's API layer requires $25K+/year and has a notoriously slow REST API with no real-time subscriptions. SiteSync's Supabase architecture with Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN provides sub-200ms real-time updates at zero additional cost. However, Procore has 300+ integrations and battle-tested webhook delivery. SiteSync's notification queue and edit lock system are architecturally sound but unfinished — the notification dispatch edge function and seed data are the critical gaps preventing any real demo or pilot.
summary: The Database & API layer has solid architectural foundations: proper project scoping middleware, typed Supabase queries, real-time subscription patterns, edit locking, audit trails, and a well-structured error hierarchy. TypeScript quality is the highest-scoring dimension at 58 thanks to consistent type guards and proper error transforms. However, the module is severely hampered by three critical gaps: (1) no seed data means every page renders empty states making demos impossible, (2) no notification queue infrastructure blocks the P0-5 email notification feature that GCs consider a dealbreaker, and (3) the activity feed only resolves labels for 2 of 7+ entity types making the dashboard activity stream mostly unreadable. The budget endpoint completeness and edit lock cleanup are secondary but important for enterprise readiness. Implementing seed data and the notification queue table are the highest-leverage actions — they unblock both frontend development and the email dispatch edge function.
